overview.title: Settings overview
overview.body: Find every workspace setting from one place. Use search to jump to billing, channels, team, tickets, or security.
tips: Only admins can change workspace-wide settings.
Your role determines which pages you can open.

custom_domain.title: Custom domain
custom_domain.body: Serve your customer portal and help center on your own domain instead of the default workspace subdomain.
tips: Add the DNS records shown here before verifying the domain.
SSL is provisioned automatically once DNS is correct.
Each brand can use the same custom domain with separate portal paths.

brands.title: About brands
brands.body: Brands let you run separate customer portals, mailboxes, and knowledge bases from one workspace. Use them for different companies, product lines, or white-label clients.
tips: Each brand can have its own portal URL, ticket prefix, colors, and default priority.
Assign email inboxes and knowledge collections per brand to keep content isolated.
Agents work from one workspace — routing and inboxes determine which brand receives each ticket.

usage_billing.title: Usage & billing
usage_billing.body: See your current plan, seat usage, ticket volume this month, and when your subscription renews.
tips: Agent seats count toward your plan limit; customer accounts do not.
Trial workspaces show remaining trial time here.
Open Payment history for past charges and receipts.

change_plan.title: Change plan
change_plan.body: Upgrade or switch your workspace subscription. Plan limits for agents, tickets, and features update when the new plan is active.
tips: You cannot downgrade to a paid plan while the free trial is still running.
Checkout is completed through Razorpay when billing is enabled.
Compare Plan features to see what each tier includes.

addons.title: Add-ons
addons.body: Enable optional capabilities such as Service Desk, AI Copilot, or Integrations on top of your base plan.
tips: Add-ons require an active paid subscription.
Some features are already included on Enterprise — check before purchasing.
Cancelled add-ons stay active until the end of the billing period.

payment_history.title: Payment history
payment_history.body: View charges and receipts for this workspace, including plan subscriptions and add-on payments.
tips: Payments appear after checkout completes or when a subscription renews.
Download or share receipts using the invoice details when available.
Contact your workspace admin if a payment is missing.

plan_features.title: Plan features
plan_features.body: See which capabilities your workspace has today based on the current plan and active add-ons.
tips: Locked features show which plan or add-on unlocks them.
Usage limits apply per billing period for tickets and seats.
Upgrade from Change plan if you need more capacity.

platform_feedback.title: Feedback
platform_feedback.body: Share suggestions, report problems, or request improvements for your workspace. Submissions go to the product team and are not visible to customers.
tips: Include steps to reproduce when reporting a bug.
Feature requests help us prioritize what to build next.
You will not receive a direct reply for every submission.

profile.title: Your profile
profile.body: Update how you appear in the workspace: name, email, avatar, language, timezone, and light or dark appearance.
tips: Timezone affects how dates and times are shown to you.
Language changes apply to your account only.
Avatar choices apply across tickets, the sidebar, and team views.

password.title: Password
password.body: Change the password you use to sign in to this workspace.
tips: Use a strong, unique password.
If SSO is required, password login may be disabled by your admin.
Enable two-factor authentication for extra protection.

two_factor.title: Two-factor authentication
two_factor.body: Protect your account with a code from an authenticator app in addition to your password.
tips: Save recovery codes in a secure place when you enable 2FA.
Your admin may require 2FA for all agents.
Use Google Authenticator, Authy, 1Password, or any TOTP app.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.body: Choose which email and in-app alerts you receive as an agent in this workspace.
tips: Ticket assigned and customer reply are the most common alerts.
SLA breach alerts help you act before deadlines pass.
These settings apply to your account, not the whole team.

customer_fields.title: Customer fields
customer_fields.body: Add custom fields to contact profiles so agents capture the data your team needs.
tips: Fields can be text, dropdown, checkbox, or date types.
Required fields apply when creating or editing customers.
Use clear labels so agents know what to collect.

ticket_numbering.title: Ticket numbering
ticket_numbering.body: Set the prefix used for new ticket IDs in this workspace, for example HD-00042.
tips: A hyphen is added automatically if you omit one.
Existing ticket numbers are not changed when you update the prefix.
Brands can override the prefix per brand in Brand settings.

ticket_statuses.title: Ticket statuses
ticket_statuses.body: Customize the statuses tickets move through from open to resolved and closed.
tips: System statuses such as Open and Closed cannot be removed.
Status order affects how tickets appear in views and reports.
Automation rules can trigger on status changes.

sla_business_hours.title: SLA & business hours
sla_business_hours.body: Define response and resolution targets and the hours when SLA clocks run for your team.
tips: Business hours pause SLA timers outside working time.
Assign SLA policies to priorities or ticket types as needed.
Breached tickets appear in reports and can trigger notifications.

auto_assignment.title: Auto-assignment
auto_assignment.body: Automatically assign new unassigned tickets to agents using round-robin, load-based, or skill-aware rules.
tips: Define agent skills under Team → Skills for skill-based routing.
Round-robin spreads work evenly across eligible agents.
Rules apply to new tickets that match your conditions.

csat_surveys.title: CSAT surveys
csat_surveys.body: Send a short satisfaction survey to customers after tickets are resolved.
tips: Surveys are typically sent by email after resolution.
Results appear in reports for quality tracking.
Keep surveys brief to improve response rates.

macros.title: Macros
macros.body: Create reusable reply snippets agents can insert into ticket responses with one click.
tips: Macros can include placeholders for customer or ticket data.
Personal macros are visible only to you; shared macros need admin setup.
Use macros for common answers to save time.

automation_rules.title: Automation rules
automation_rules.body: Run actions automatically when tickets are created or updated — assign, tag, change status, or notify.
tips: Rules are evaluated in order; place specific rules before general ones.
Test with a narrow condition before rolling out widely.
Available on plans that include automation.

ticket_fields.title: Ticket fields
ticket_fields.body: Add custom fields to tickets so agents record structured information on every request.
tips: Fields can appear on the agent ticket form and optionally the portal.
Dropdown fields need options defined before use.
Use fields in automation conditions where supported.

external_issues.title: External issues
external_issues.body: Control whether ticket status syncs from linked Jira or Linear issues when engineers update them externally.
tips: Inbound sync is off by default — enable only if you want helpefi to follow the external tool.
Link issues from the ticket sidebar after connecting Jira or Linear.
Outbound updates from helpefi to Jira/Linear still work when integrations are connected.

jira.title: Jira
jira.body: Connect Jira Cloud so agents link tickets to issues and keep engineering work in sync.
tips: Use a Jira API token with access to the projects you need.
Linked issues appear on the ticket sidebar for quick context.
Configure external issue sync under Ticket settings if needed.

linear.title: Linear
linear.body: Connect Linear so agents can link tickets to issues and track engineering work alongside support.
tips: Generate a Linear API key with issue read and write access.
Link issues from the ticket view after connecting.
Status sync options are under External issues in Ticket settings.

incoming_email.title: Incoming email
incoming_email.body: Connect mailboxes that create tickets when customers send email to your support addresses.
tips: Each inbox can be tied to a brand and default routing rules.
OAuth providers simplify setup for Gmail and Microsoft 365.
IMAP is available for other providers.

outgoing_email.title: Outgoing email
outgoing_email.body: Configure SMTP so agent replies to customers are sent from your support addresses.
tips: Match the From address to the inbox customers originally emailed.
Test SMTP after saving to confirm delivery.
Use Advanced email for SPF and DKIM guidance.

advanced_email.title: Advanced email
advanced_email.body: Improve deliverability with SPF, DKIM, and policies for how your workspace handles inbound and outbound mail.
tips: Publish DNS records exactly as shown to avoid spam folders.
Blocklist addresses you do not want creating tickets.
Changes may take up to 48 hours to propagate in DNS.

email_auto_reply.title: Email auto-reply
email_auto_reply.body: Send an automatic first response when a new ticket arrives by email, and manage inbound blocklists.
tips: Keep auto-replies short and set expectations on response time.
Block spam or internal addresses that should not create tickets.
Auto-reply applies to new conversations, not every message.

email_templates.title: Email templates
email_templates.body: Customize subject lines and HTML bodies for automated emails your workspace sends to customers and agents.
tips: Templates cover notifications such as ticket created or password reset.
Use variables documented in each template for dynamic content.
Preview before saving wide-reaching changes.

ticket_sources.title: Ticket sources
ticket_sources.body: Manage how customers reach you beyond email: web widget, portal forms, API, and other intake channels.
tips: Each source can create tickets with different default fields or routing.
Disable sources you are not using to reduce noise.
Embed the chat widget on your site for live conversations.

whatsapp_sms.title: WhatsApp & SMS
whatsapp_sms.body: Connect Twilio to receive and reply to WhatsApp and SMS messages as tickets in this workspace.
tips: You need a Twilio account with a WhatsApp-enabled number or SMS sender.
Messages appear in the inbox like other channel conversations.
Configure webhooks in Twilio using the values shown here.

webhooks.title: Webhooks
webhooks.body: Send real-time HTTP notifications to your systems when ticket events occur in this workspace.
tips: Subscribe only to the events your integration needs.
Store the signing secret securely to verify payloads.
Failed deliveries are retried according to your endpoint reliability.

slack.title: Slack
slack.body: Post ticket notifications to Slack channels and let agents reply from threads where configured.
tips: Install the app to the Slack workspace your team uses daily.
Choose channels per brand or team as needed.
Requires the integrations feature on your plan.

shopify.title: Shopify
shopify.body: Show Shopify order and customer context on tickets for your ecommerce support team.
tips: Connect the Shopify store your customers buy from.
Order details appear when the customer email matches a Shopify account.
Useful for refunds, shipping, and order status questions.

hubspot.title: HubSpot
hubspot.body: Enrich tickets with HubSpot contact and company data for your sales and support handoffs.
tips: Match customers by email address on the ticket.
Requires a HubSpot private app token with contact read access.
Data is read-only in helpefi unless you build automations via webhooks.

salesforce.title: Salesforce
salesforce.body: Pull Salesforce account and contact context into tickets for B2B support teams.
tips: Use a connected app with API access to the objects you need.
Linking works best when customer emails match Salesforce contacts.
Sensitive CRM data follows your Salesforce permissions.

microsoft_teams.title: Microsoft Teams
microsoft_teams.body: Send ticket notifications to Teams channels so your team stays informed without leaving Microsoft 365.
tips: Connect the Teams tenant your company uses.
Map channels to brands or queues as needed.
Notifications include links back to the ticket in helpefi.

zapier.title: Zapier
zapier.body: Connect helpefi to thousands of apps using Zapier triggers and actions for this workspace.
tips: Common zaps: create tickets from forms, sync to spreadsheets, or notify chat tools.
Use your workspace API key in Zapier authentication.
Test zaps with a single ticket before enabling for all traffic.

agents.title: Agents
agents.body: Manage who can access this workspace, their roles, and team membership.
tips: Admins can change all settings; agents handle tickets within their permissions.
Seat limits depend on your subscription plan.
Deactivate users instead of deleting to preserve ticket history attribution.

invitations.title: Invitations
invitations.body: Invite new agents or admins by email. They receive a link to join this workspace.
tips: Pending invites count toward seat limits on some plans.
Resend or revoke invites from this list.
Choose the role at invite time — change it later under Roles if needed.

teams_departments.title: Teams & departments
teams_departments.body: Organize agents into departments and teams for routing, reporting, and ownership.
tips: Assign a team lead or department head for escalations.
Auto-assignment rules can target specific teams.
Agents can belong to multiple teams.

skills.title: Skills
skills.body: Tag agents with skills such as language or product area so assignment rules route tickets to the right people.
tips: Skills are used by auto-assignment when skill-based routing is enabled.
Keep the skill list short and meaningful.
Update skills when team responsibilities change.

roles_permissions.title: Roles & permissions
roles_permissions.body: Control what each role can view and change across tickets, settings, and reports in this workspace.
tips: Built-in roles cover most teams; customize only when necessary.
Restrict settings access to admins for security.
Permission changes apply immediately to all users with that role.

agent_fields.title: Agent fields
agent_fields.body: Add custom fields to agent profiles for internal metadata such as region, shift, or employee ID.
tips: Agent fields are visible to admins, not to customers.
Useful for workforce planning and routing.
Fields appear when editing team members.

security_overview.title: Security overview
security_overview.body: Review security recommendations and the current posture of this workspace.
tips: Address high-priority items such as MFA and SSO first.
Only workspace admins can change security policy.
Audit logs complement this view for forensic review.

security_policy.title: Security policy
security_policy.body: Set password rules, session timeouts, and whether agents must use two-factor authentication.
tips: Stricter policies improve security but may require user onboarding.
Session length affects how often agents must sign in again.
Changes apply to new sessions after saving.

single_sign_on.title: Single sign-on
single_sign_on.body: Let agents sign in with your company identity provider using SAML or OIDC.
tips: Collect IdP metadata from your IT team before configuring.
Test with one admin account before enforcing for all agents.
SSO is typically available on Enterprise plans.

data_retention.title: Data retention
data_retention.body: Define how long tickets, attachments, and audit data are kept in this workspace.
tips: Longer retention helps compliance but uses more storage.
Some regions require minimum retention periods — check with legal.
Deletion is permanent after the retention period ends.

audit_logs.title: Audit logs
audit_logs.body: View a tamper-evident history of important changes made in this workspace and who made them.
tips: Filter by user, action, or date range to investigate incidents.
Export logs for compliance reviews when needed.
Access requires the audit.view permission.

ai_assistant.title: AI assistant
ai_assistant.body: Configure AI features such as suggested replies, triage, and knowledge search for agents in this workspace.
tips: AI uses your knowledge base and ticket context — keep articles up to date.
Review suggestions before sending to customers when quality matters.
Requires the AI feature or AI Copilot add-on on your plan.

service_catalog.title: Service catalog
service_catalog.body: Build categories and requestable items customers can order from the portal, such as hardware or access requests.
tips: Each item can have its own form fields and approval flow.
Publish only items your team is ready to fulfill.
Catalog requests create tickets for tracking.

change_approvals.title: Change approvals
change_approvals.body: Define who must approve change requests before work proceeds in Service Desk.
tips: Approvers receive notifications when their sign-off is needed.
Use approval groups for CAB-style reviews.
Requires the Service Desk add-on.

api_docs.title: API docs
api_docs.body: Explore the REST API for this workspace to create tickets, manage customers, and build integrations programmatically.
tips: Authenticate with an API token from your agent profile or admin settings.
Rate limits apply per workspace — batch requests when possible.
Use webhooks for real-time events instead of polling when you can.
